Manual Overview

Source: vignettes/manual-index.Rmd
manual-index.Rmd

DPmixGPD User Manual

Welcome to the DPmixGPD user manual. This section provides comprehensive documentation for using the package effectively.

Getting Started

Begin here if you’re new to DPmixGPD:

  • Introduction - Quick start guide with basic examples to get you up and running

User Guide

Detailed documentation organized by topic:

Core Concepts

  • Available Distributions - Overview of supported mixture kernels (Normal, Lognormal, Gamma, etc.) and their properties

  • Model Specification - How to specify models, set priors, and configure parameters

  • MCMC Workflow - Running MCMC chains, convergence diagnostics, and best practices

Model Types

  • Unconditional Models - Density estimation without covariates for univariate data

  • Conditional Models - Covariate-dependent density estimation using parameter linking

  • Causal Inference - Treatment effect estimation with propensity score adjustment

  • Umbrella: Custom Models (All-in-One) - Consolidated custom model recipes for CRP/SB, GPD, and causal workflows

  • Customization: Build NIMBLE Models from Scratch - Manual SB/CRP workflows using exported nimbleFunctions with full NIMBLE pipelines

Advanced Topics

  • Backends (CRP vs SB) - Comparing Chinese Restaurant Process and Stick-Breaking inference approaches

  • S3 Methods Reference - Complete guide to using print(), summary(), plot(), predict(), and other S3 methods

How to Use This Manual

  1. New users: Start with the Introduction for a quick overview
  2. Building models: Read Model Specification and MCMC Workflow
  3. Specific use cases: Jump to Unconditional, Conditional, or Causal based on your needs
  4. API details: See S3 Methods Reference for comprehensive method documentation

NIMBLE Version Notes

DPmixGPD is developed and tested with nimble 1.4.0 (see renv.lock). Other recent nimble releases should work, but if you encounter compilation or sampler issues, please match the tested version first.

On this page

Developed by Arnab Aich.
